mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4% annually, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which improves near-term corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat following INDPRO increases during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when volatility index exceeds 25.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals weakening construction demand and reduced household wealth effects, which depresses consumer spending forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in > 60% of cases following a >5% HOUST drop, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a stable unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength without overheating, which sustains consumer spending capacity and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 300k monthly, or the interaction effect shows negative out-of-sample IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in delayed monetary tightening and margin compression, which immediately penalizes equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive following CPIAUCSL acceleration during inflationary surges, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue baselines and improves earnings per share forecasts, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.
